# Typing Indicators

> Real-time indicators showing which users are currently typing, recording, uploading, or performing other in-progress actions in a conversation

**Category:** Communication · **Version:** 1.0.0 · **Tags:** typing · real-time · notifications · activity · presence

## What this does

Real-time indicators showing which users are currently typing, recording, uploading, or performing other in-progress actions in a conversation

Specifies 6 acceptance outcomes that any implementation must satisfy, regardless of language or framework.

## Fields

- **room_id** *(text, required)* — Room ID
- **user_id** *(text, required)* — User ID
- **username** *(text, required)* — Username
- **activity_type** *(select, required)* — Activity Type
- **thread_id** *(text, optional)* — Thread ID
- **timeout_ms** *(number, optional)* — Activity Timeout (ms)

## What must be true

- **general:** Activity signals auto-expire after a configurable timeout (default 15 seconds) if not renewed, Clients must renew the activity signal at regular intervals (approximately one-third of the timeout period) while the activity continues, Each room and thread tracks activity independently; thread activities are scoped to the thread ID, A user should not receive their own activity indicator in the UI, Multiple activity types can be active simultaneously for the same user in the same room, Activity notifications are broadcast to all room subscribers except the originating user, Display name shown in indicators follows the system-wide name display preference (username vs real name), Activity signals use debouncing to avoid flooding the broadcast channel, Continuously-performing activities (recording, uploading) must use a renewal interval to keep the indicator visible, When a user leaves a room or disconnects, all their active indicators for that room must be cleared

## Success & failure scenarios

**✅ Success paths**

- **Activity Started** — when user begins an in-progress activity in a room; room_id exists; activity_type exists, then Activity indicator is broadcast to all other participants in the room.
- **Activity Renewed** — when user sends a renewal signal before the previous signal expires; activity_status eq "active", then Activity indicator timer is reset; indicator remains visible to other participants.
- **Activity Stopped Explicitly** — when user submits message, cancels action, or explicitly stops activity; activity_status eq "active", then Activity indicator is cleared for this user in this room.
- **Activity Expired** — when activity timeout elapses with no renewal signal received; activity_status eq "active", then Stale activity indicator is automatically cleared for all subscribers.
- **Own Activity Suppressed** — when subscriber receives an activity event for their own user ID, then Activity indicator is not displayed to the originating user.

**❌ Failure paths**

- **Unauthenticated Activity** — when user_id not_exists, then Activity signal rejected because the sender is not authenticated. *(error: `TYPING_NOT_AUTHENTICATED`)*

## Errors it can return

- `TYPING_NOT_AUTHENTICATED` — You must be signed in to send activity indicators

## Connects to

- **user-presence** *(recommended)* — Presence status provides broader availability context alongside typing indicators
- **direct-messaging** *(optional)* — Typing indicators are commonly used in direct message conversations
- **channel-messaging** *(optional)* — Typing indicators are commonly used in channel conversations
